text.title: ICP Message Mining from AI Conversations Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every ICP Message Mining from AI Conversations application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Mining begins only after consent + privacy basis is documented. PII redacted at ingestion; not at analysis.
rule.rationale: Consent failures = regulatory + reputational risk; redact-on-ingest is the only safe design.

rule.statement: Use a fixed taxonomy: JTBD phrase, objection, success quote, churn signal, competitor mention. Free-form mining produces noise.
rule.rationale: Without fixed taxonomy, multiple miners produce incompatible outputs; taxonomy creates aggregation.

rule.statement: Every extracted item retains the verbatim quote + source ID. Paraphrased extractions are LLM-generated noise.
rule.rationale: Paraphrased extractions cannot be cited in copy; verbatim is the only legally + creatively useful form.

rule.statement: Monthly synthesis: top 5 JTBD phrases, top 5 objections, top 5 success quotes. Feeds marketing copy + product roadmap.
rule.rationale: Without synthesis cadence, transcripts pile up; monthly forces compounding.

rule.statement: Synthesis feeds at least 1 copy change per month (landing page hero, FAQ, ad headline). Without loop, mining is research-theatre.
rule.rationale: Mining without copy change is busy-work; the loop is the value.
